info.name: Martial Levels
info.description: Martial Levels Class Features introduced in Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms
info.author: Loot Tavern

element.name: RGTTY Advanced Weapon Masteries
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Advanced weapon mastery is a system that grants new techniques to warriors, representing their dedication to combat and skill with a favoured weapon property. It opens up new pathways for players to explore creativity, flavour, and gameplay options, while also reducing some of the power imbalance between martial and spellcasting classes that can emerge at higher levels.

setters.set: You can introduce Advanced Weapon Masteries to your campaign.

element.name: Advanced Weapon Masteries
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Martial Level & Multiclassing. Your martial level dictates the levels at which you unlock advanced weapon mastery advanced techniques. You determine your martial level by adding together all your levels in the full-martial classes and half your levels in half-martial classes (rounded down). For example, a character that has multiclassed as a fighter 3/ranger 7 has a martial level of 6.

Advanced Techniques. Advanced techniques are special manoeuvres associated with a weapon property. A character can perform a technique with any weapon that possesses that property, provided the character is proficient with that weapon, has learned the technique, and meets its prerequisites. For example, a character that chooses the Dizzying Strike advanced technique from the Impact technique tree can perform the Dizzying Strike with any weapon that has the Impact property with which they are proficient.

Saving Throws.Whenever you use an advanced technique that forces a target to make a saving throw, it is against your martial save DC, which is calculated using any modifiers to your attack rolls with the weapon you are using to perform the technique. This includes your proficiency bonus, your ability modifier (typically Strength or Dexterity, but effects like the shillelagh cantrip could change this), attack roll bonuses if the weapon is magical, or other effects currently affecting you or the weapon. It does not include any bonuses that rely on die rolls, such as the effect of the bless spell or Bardic Inspiration.

center.p: Martial save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your ability modifier + other attack roll bonuses

description.p: Critical Hit Threshold.Some of the advanced techniques in this chapter reduce the critical hit threshold of an attack. Usually, a critical hit is scored on a roll of a 20 on the d20. When a technique (such as the Heavy tree's Executioner) says "your critical hit threshold... is reduced by 1", this means you score a critical hit on a 19 or 20. These effects stack with other advanced techniques, class features, and combo attacks.

For example, a character that takes the Steady Loader and Dead-Eye advanced techniques from the Loading tree scores a critical hit on a roll of 17-20 when they make an attack roll with a Loading weapon. A Champion fighter gets the Improved Critical class feature at 3rd level, letting them score a critical hit on a 19 or 20. If they also take the Executioner advanced technique from the Heavy tree, they score a critical hit on a roll of 18-20 when they make an attack with a Heavy weapon against a prone creature.

When you reach martial level 2, you learn one tier 1 advanced technique. As you increase in martial level, you learn more techniques, including more powerful ones at higher tiers, as shown in the Advanced Weapon Mastery Progression table.

Technique Trees.Advanced techniques are split into twelve technique trees, each of which is associated with one property: Ammunition, Finesse, Heavy, Impact*, Light, Loading, Pugilist*, Reach, Scourge*, Shield**, Thrown, and Versatile. Each technique tree has four tiers; to pick an advanced technique from tier 2 or higher, you must know at least one advanced technique from a lower tier in that property’s technique tree. For example, to pick the tier 4 Finesse advanced technique Opportunist, you must have already chosen one of the tier 3 techniques, either Jink or Weak Spot.
In addition, advanced techniques have martial level prerequisites as shown on each technique tree and in the Advanced Weapon Mastery Progression table.

Optional Rule: Level Up.When you gain a level, you can replace one of the advanced techniques you know with another of your choice for which you meet the prerequisites. You can only do so if this doesn’t remove the prerequisite for another technique you know.

sheet.description: • Your Martial Level is: {{martial-level}} • Your Martial Save is: {{martial-save:dc}} + other attack roll bonuses • You Know {{techniques-known}} Techniques (Max Tier: {{max-tier-techniques}}) • Critical Hit Threshold: {{critical-threshold}} - 20 • Superior Strike: {{proficiency}}/Long Rest (1/Turn)

element.name: Full Martial Masteries
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

p.i: This item take your class as a Full Martial according Advanced Weapon Masteries rule from Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms. You can equip this item to enable it. It remains hidden from the inventory on your character sheet.

element.name: Half Martial Masteries
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

p.i: This item take your class as a Half Martial according Advanced Weapon Masteries rule from Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms. You can equip this item to enable it. It remains hidden from the inventory on your character sheet.

element.name: Ammunition Mastery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Several Ammunition advanced techniques let you modify ammunition. A single piece of ammunition can only have a single modification. If it is modified for a second time, it loses any previous modifications it had.

Ammunition Property. You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition.Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield. If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon. A sling must be loaded to deal any damage when used in this way.

element.name: Ammunition Mastery (1)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Several Ammunition advanced techniques let you modify ammunition. A single piece of ammunition can only have a single modification. If it is modified for a second time, it loses any previous modifications it had.

element.name: Aerodynamic Ammunition
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you finish a short or long rest, you can modify a number of pieces of ammunition equal to twice your proficiency bonus, increasing their aerodynamism. The normal range of attack rolls you make using weapons that use this ammunition is doubled, and the long range increases by half its value. Any specialised ammunition you create using this technique deteriorates 24 hours after it's created.

element.name: Tinkered Artillery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you finish a short or long rest, you can modify a number of pieces of ammunition equal to your proficiency bonus. When you modify a piece of ammunition, choose from the options below
Poisonous. On a hit, the target takes an extra 1d6 poison damage and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the end of your next turn.
Whomping. On a hit, the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

element.name: Superior Strike: Ammunition
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can perform a superior strike with any weapon that has the Ammunition property.

element.name: Double Load
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Once on your turn, when you make an attack with an Ammunition weapon, you can load two pieces of ammunition into the weapon. If the attack hits, it deals one extra die of the weapon's damage. This technique can't be used with Loading's Twin Load.

element.name: Tooled Artillery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you finish a short or long rest, you can modify a number of pieces of ammunition equal to your proficiency bonus, choosing from the options below.

Explosive. Hit or miss, the target and each creature within 5 feet of it must make a Dexterity saving throw. A target that is hit by your attack automatically fails this saving throw. A creature takes 1d8 thunder damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success.
Smoke Bomb. Hit or miss, the ammunition erupts in a cloud of smoke. The area within 10 feet of the target is heavily obscured until the start of your next turn.

When you make an attack with an Ammunition weapon, you can use one of your modified pieces of ammunition, but you can only target creatures or objects within that weapon's normal range for that attack roll. Any specialised ammunition you create using this technique deteriorates 24 hours after it's created.

element.name: Rapid Fire
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Once per turn, after you take the Attack action and make an attack with an Ammunition weapon, you can make a DC 15 Dexterity check. On a success, you can make one additional attack with that weapon as part of the same action.

element.name: Runed Artillery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you finish a short or long rest, you can modify a number of pieces of ammunition equal to your proficiency bonus, choosing from the options below.
Razored. You have advantage on attack rolls made using this piece of ammunition, and your critical hit threshold is reduced by 1 when making attack rolls with it.

Phosphorous. On a hit, the target takes an extra 3d6 fire damage, emits bright light for 10 feet and dim light for an additional 10 feet, and can't benefit from being invisible or taking the Hide action. The phosphorus burns for 1 minute, or until the target uses an action to extinguish the flame or is submerged in water.

When you make an attack with an Ammunition weapon, you can use one of your modified pieces of ammunition, but you can only target creatures or objects within that weapon's normal range for that attack roll. Any specialised ammunition you create using this technique deteriorates 24 hours after it's created.

element.name: Finesse Mastery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Finesse Property. When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

element.name: Finesse Mastery (1)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Finesse Property. When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

element.name: Finesse Mastery (2)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Finesse Property. When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

element.name: Finesse Mastery (3)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Finesse Property. When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

element.name: Ammunition Mastery (4)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Finesse Property. When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

element.name: Ammunition Mastery (5)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Finesse Property. When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

element.name: Ammunition Mastery (6)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Finesse Property. When making an attack with a Finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

element.name: Ambidextrous
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you take the Attack action and attack with a Finesse melee weapon that you're holding in one hand, you can use a bonus action to attack with a different Finesse melee weapon that you're holding in the other hand. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus attack, unless that modifier is negative.

element.name: Nimble Duelist
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you hit a creature with a melee attack using a Finesse weapon, that creature can't make opportunity attacks against you until the end of that turn.

element.name: Superior Strike: Finesse
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can perform a superior strike with any weapon that has the Finesse property.

element.name: Jink
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you take the Dodge action, you can make one melee attack with a Finesse weapon as a part of that action, but you take a -4 penalty to the attack roll.

element.name: Weak Spot
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you make an attack with a Finesse weapon with advantage, you can deal extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus on a hit. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.

element.name: Fighting Elegance
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Your AC increases by 2 while you're wielding a Finesse weapon and not wielding a non-Finesse weapon or shield in your other hand.

element.name: Opportunist
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When a creature within 5 feet of you attacks a creature other than you, you can use your reaction to make a melee attack against it with a Finesse weapon.

element.name: Heavy Mastery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Heavy advance techniques apply only to melee weapons with the Heavy property. For ranged weapon advanced techniques, reference the Ammunition and Loading & Reload masteries.
Heavy Property. Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small creature to use effectively.

element.name: Heavy Mastery (1)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Heavy advance techniques apply only to melee weapons with the Heavy property. For ranged weapon advanced techniques, reference the Ammunition and Loading & Reload masteries.
Heavy Property. Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small creature to use effectively.

Heavy Property. Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small creature to use effectively.

element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Heavy advance techniques apply only to melee weapons with the Heavy property. For ranged weapon advanced techniques, reference the Ammunition and Loading & Reload masteries.
Heavy Property. Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small creature to use effectively.

element.name: Colossal Strength
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: If your Strength score is 15 or higher, being Small or Tiny doesn't impose disadvantage on your attack rolls with Heavy weapons.

element.name: Executioner
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you hit a prone creature with an attack using a Heavy weapon, the weapon deals one extra die of damage. Additionally, your critical hit threshold for such attacks is reduced by 1.

element.name: Hammering Blow
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you hit a creature no more than one size larger than you with a Heavy weapon and roll the highest result on the weapon's damage dice, you can knock the target prone.

element.name: Superior Strike: Heavy
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can perform a superior strike with any weapon that has the Heavy property.

element.name: Cleave
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with a melee attack roll using a Heavy weapon, you can choose to carry over any excess damage to one other creature within your reach, provided the initial attack roll would hit the target. If you reduce that creature to 0 hit points, you can repeat this process against another target until there is no excess damage remaining.

element.name: Dauntless
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you roll a 1 on a bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage die when you hit with a melee attack with a Heavy weapon, treat that 1 as the maximum roll for that die.

element.name: Concussive Blow
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you score a critical hit on an attack with a Heavy weapon, the target also suffers the effect of the confusion spell until the end of its next turn.

element.name: Staggering Blow
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you hit a creature no more than one size larger than you with a Heavy weapon, you can push the target up to 10 feet directly away from you. Creatures immune to the grappled condition are immune to this effect.

element.name: Impact Mastery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Impact Property. This weapon deals double damage to objects and structures.

element.name: Impact Mastery (1)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Impact Property. This weapon deals double damage to objects and structures.

element.name: Impact Mastery (2)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Impact Property. This weapon deals double damage to objects and structures.

element.name: Impact Mastery (3)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Impact Property. This weapon deals double damage to objects and structures.

element.name: Impact Mastery (4)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Impact Property. This weapon deals double damage to objects and structures.

element.name: Impact Mastery (5)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Impact Property. This weapon deals double damage to objects and structures.

element.name: Impact Mastery (6)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Impact Property. This weapon deals double damage to objects and structures.

element.name: Direct Damage
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Your damage rolls for attacks you make with Impact weapons ignore temporary hit points, striking at a target's normal hit points directly. In addition, if an attack you make with an Impact weapon forces a target to make a Constitution saving throw to maintain its concentration, it has disadvantage on the saving throw.

element.name: Dizzying Strike
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack using an Impact weapon, that creature's speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn. This effect can stack.

element.name: Superior Strike: Impact
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can perform a superior strike with any weapon that has the Impact property.

element.name: Momentum Swing
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you take the Attack action and miss a target with an Impact weapon, you can immediately use a bonus action to make another attack with that weapon against the target. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus attack, unless that modifier is negative.

element.name: Staggering Strike
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you hit a creature with an Impact weapon and roll the highest result on the weapon's damage dice or score a critical hit, that creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be staggered until the end of its next turn. While staggered, a creature has disadvantage on all attack rolls and on ability checks and saving throws that use Dexterity or Intelligence, and it can't take reactions.

element.name: Crushing Force
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You have advantage on melee attack rolls with Impact weapons against creatures at least one size category smaller than you.

element.name: Shattered Steel
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you roll the maximum on your damage roll when you hit a creature with an Impact weapon, you push the force of that strike onto an object (such as a weapon) the creature is holding. The target must make a Strength (Athletics) check against your martial save DC. On a success, it drops the object. On a failure, the object shatters. If the object is a magic item of a rarity equal to or higher than that of your weapon, the target automatically succeeds on this check.

element.name: Light Mastery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Light Property. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light melee weapon that you're holding in one hand, you can use a bonus action to attack with a different Light melee weapon that you're holding in the other hand. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus attack, unless that modifier is negative. If either weapon also has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon instead of making a melee attack with it.

element.name: Light Mastery (1)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Light Property. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light melee weapon that you're holding in one hand, you can use a bonus action to attack with a different Light melee weapon that you're holding in the other hand. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus attack, unless that modifier is negative. If either weapon also has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon instead of making a melee attack with it.

element.name: Light Mastery (2)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Light Property. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light melee weapon that you're holding in one hand, you can use a bonus action to attack with a different Light melee weapon that you're holding in the other hand. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus attack, unless that modifier is negative. If either weapon also has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon instead of making a melee attack with it.

element.name: Light Mastery (3)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Light Property. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light melee weapon that you're holding in one hand, you can use a bonus action to attack with a different Light melee weapon that you're holding in the other hand. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus attack, unless that modifier is negative. If either weapon also has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon instead of making a melee attack with it.

element.name: Light Mastery (4)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Light Property. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light melee weapon that you're holding in one hand, you can use a bonus action to attack with a different Light melee weapon that you're holding in the other hand. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus attack, unless that modifier is negative. If either weapon also has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon instead of making a melee attack with it.

element.name: Light Mastery (5)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Light Property. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light melee weapon that you're holding in one hand, you can use a bonus action to attack with a different Light melee weapon that you're holding in the other hand. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus attack, unless that modifier is negative. If either weapon also has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon instead of making a melee attack with it.

element.name: Light Mastery (6)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Light Property. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light melee weapon that you're holding in one hand, you can use a bonus action to attack with a different Light melee weapon that you're holding in the other hand. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus attack, unless that modifier is negative. If either weapon also has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon instead of making a melee attack with it.

element.name: Lightweight
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: While the only weapons you are wielding are Light weapons and you aren't wearing a shield or heavy armour, your speed increases by 10 feet.

element.name: Multi-Weapon Fighting
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you engage in two-weapon fighting, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus action attack if the weapon is Light.

element.name: Superior Strike: Light
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can perform a superior strike with any weapon that has the Light property.

element.name: Combination Strike
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light weapon, any attack roll you make as a bonus action this turn with a Light weapon has advantage.

element.name: Riposte
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you are wielding two Light weapons and a creature within your reach misses you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to make an attack against that creature with one of the weapons.

element.name: Featherweight
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: While the only weapons you are wielding are Light weapons and you aren't wearing a shield or heavy armour, you gain a +1 bonus to AC and have advantage on Dexterity saving throws.

element.name: Swift Striker
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you take the Attack action, if the only weapons you are wielding are Light weapons, you can make one additional attack with a Light weapon as a part of that action, but you do not add your ability modifier to the damage, unless that modifier is negative.

element.name: Loading & Reload Mastery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: This advanced weapon mastery affects weapons with either the Loading or Reload property.

Loading Property. Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Reload (X). A limited number of shots, specified by the number in parentheses after the Reload property, can be made with this weapon. A character must then reload it using an action.

element.name: Loading & Reload Mastery (1)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: This advanced weapon mastery affects weapons with either the Loading or Reload property.

Loading Property. Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Reload (X). A limited number of shots, specified by the number in parentheses after the Reload property, can be made with this weapon. A character must then reload it using an action.

element.name: Loading & Reload Mastery (2)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: This advanced weapon mastery affects weapons with either the Loading or Reload property.

Loading Property. Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Reload (X). A limited number of shots, specified by the number in parentheses after the Reload property, can be made with this weapon. A character must then reload it using an action.

element.name: Loading & Reload Mastery (3)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: This advanced weapon mastery affects weapons with either the Loading or Reload property.

Loading Property. Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Reload (X). A limited number of shots, specified by the number in parentheses after the Reload property, can be made with this weapon. A character must then reload it using an action.

element.name: Loading & Reload Mastery (4)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: This advanced weapon mastery affects weapons with either the Loading or Reload property.

Loading Property. Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Reload (X). A limited number of shots, specified by the number in parentheses after the Reload property, can be made with this weapon. A character must then reload it using an action.

element.name: Loading & Reload Mastery (5)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: This advanced weapon mastery affects weapons with either the Loading or Reload property.

Loading Property. Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Reload (X). A limited number of shots, specified by the number in parentheses after the Reload property, can be made with this weapon. A character must then reload it using an action.

element.name: Loading & Reload Mastery (6)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: This advanced weapon mastery affects weapons with either the Loading or Reload property.

Loading Property. Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Reload (X). A limited number of shots, specified by the number in parentheses after the Reload property, can be made with this weapon. A character must then reload it using an action.

element.name: Flexible Weaponry
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can use a Loading or Reload weapon as a crude club. It is a simple melee weapon with the Finesse property that deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage on a hit. If you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using a Loading or Reload weapon, that creature can't make opportunity attacks against you until the start of its next turn.

element.name: Hyper Focus
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: As a bonus action, you can steady your body and mind to give yourself advantage on the next attack roll you make with a Loading or Reload weapon this turn.

element.name: Speed Loader
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You ignore the Loading property of weapons. You can reload a weapon with the Reload property using either an action or bonus action.

element.name: Superior Strike: Loading
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can perform a superior strike with any weapon that has the Loading or Reload property.

element.name: Steady Loader
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you take the Attack action, you can choose to make only one attack with a Loading or Reload weapon with that action. If you do so, the critical hit threshold for that attack is reduced by 1 for each attack you could've normally made with that action. For example, if you normally score a critical hit on a roll of 19-20 and can attack twice when you take the Attack action, you score a critical hit on a roll of 17-20 when using this mastery.

element.name: Twin Load
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Once per turn, when you take the Attack action, you can forgo one of the attacks you can make as part of that action to load a second piece of ammunition. If the next attack you make with that weapon hits, your attack deals extra damage equal to your weapon's damage die plus your proficiency bonus. This technique can't be used with Ammunition's Double Load.

element.name: Dead-Eye
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: The critical hit threshold for attack rolls you make with Loading or Reload weapons is reduced by 1.

element.name: Quick Shot
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: After you take the Attack action and only attack with a Loading or Reload weapon, you can make one attack with that weapon as a bonus action that turn. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus attack, unless that modifier is negative.

element.name: Pugilist Mastery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Pugilist Property. For creatures that roll a die when they make unarmed strikes (such as the monk or Tavern Brawler fighter from L'Arsene's Ledger), the size of their damage die is increased by one (maximum 1d12) when they make unarmed strikes with a hand equipped with a weapon that has this property.

element.name: Pugilist Mastery (1)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Pugilist Property. For creatures that roll a die when they make unarmed strikes (such as the monk or Tavern Brawler fighter from L'Arsene's Ledger), the size of their damage die is increased by one (maximum 1d12) when they make unarmed strikes with a hand equipped with a weapon that has this property.

element.name: Pugilist Mastery (2)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Pugilist Property. For creatures that roll a die when they make unarmed strikes (such as the monk or Tavern Brawler fighter from L'Arsene's Ledger), the size of their damage die is increased by one (maximum 1d12) when they make unarmed strikes with a hand equipped with a weapon that has this property.

element.name: Pugilist Mastery (3)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Pugilist Property. For creatures that roll a die when they make unarmed strikes (such as the monk or Tavern Brawler fighter from L'Arsene's Ledger), the size of their damage die is increased by one (maximum 1d12) when they make unarmed strikes with a hand equipped with a weapon that has this property.

element.name: Pugilist Mastery (4)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Pugilist Property. For creatures that roll a die when they make unarmed strikes (such as the monk or Tavern Brawler fighter from L'Arsene's Ledger), the size of their damage die is increased by one (maximum 1d12) when they make unarmed strikes with a hand equipped with a weapon that has this property.

element.name: Pugilist Mastery (5)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Pugilist Property. For creatures that roll a die when they make unarmed strikes (such as the monk or Tavern Brawler fighter from L'Arsene's Ledger), the size of their damage die is increased by one (maximum 1d12) when they make unarmed strikes with a hand equipped with a weapon that has this property.

element.name: Pugilist Mastery (6)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Pugilist Property. For creatures that roll a die when they make unarmed strikes (such as the monk or Tavern Brawler fighter from L'Arsene's Ledger), the size of their damage die is increased by one (maximum 1d12) when they make unarmed strikes with a hand equipped with a weapon that has this property.

element.name: Combination
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: The second time on your turn that you hit the same creature with an unarmed strike or an attack with a Pugilist weapon, you deal extra damage to it equal to your proficiency bonus.

element.name: Trained Pugilist
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can roll a d6 in place of the normal damage of your unarmed strikes. Attacks with Pugilist weapons increase this damage die by one size to a d8. Your unarmed strikes and attacks made with Pugilist weapons count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistances and immunities to nonmagical attacks and damage.

element.name: Uppercut Specialist
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you attack a creature at least one size larger than you with an unarmed strike or a Pugilist weapon, your critical hit threshold is reduced by 1.

element.name: Welterweight
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for the attack and damage rolls of your unarmed strikes or Pugilist weapons.

element.name: Superior Strike: Pugilist
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can perform a superior strike with any weapon that has the Pugilist property and can perform the Flurry superior strike if you attack with an unarmed strike.

element.name: Blitzer
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: After you take the Attack action and only attack with unarmed strikes or Pugilist weapons, you can make one unarmed strike or attack with a Pugilist weapon as a bonus action that turn. You can replace this attack with an attempt to grapple or shove a creature.

element.name: Expert Pugilist
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can roll a d8 in place of the normal damage of your unarmed strikes. Attacks with Pugilist weapons increase this damage die by one size to a d10. Your unarmed strikes and attacks made with Pugilist weapons count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistances and immunities to nonmagical attacks and damage.

element.name: Finisher
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: The third time on your turn that you hit the same creature with an unarmed strike or Pugilist weapon, you deal extra damage to it equal to your ability modifier used for the attack.

element.name: Got 'em
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You have advantage on opportunity attacks made with unarmed strikes or with Pugilist weapons, and when a creature provokes an opportunity attack from you, you can attempt to grapple that creature as your opportunity attack.

element.name: Fair Fighter
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an unarmed strike or attack with a Pugilist weapon, you can force the creature to make a Strength saving throw against your martial save DC. On a failure, it drops one weapon or other object it is holding (your choice). The object lands in your space.

element.name: Master Pugilist
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can roll a d10 in place of the normal damage of your unarmed strikes. Attacks with Pugilist weapons increase this damage die by one size to a d12. Your unarmed strikes and attacks made with Pugilist weapons count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistances and immunities to nonmagical attacks and damage.

element.name: Suplex King
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When a creature no more than one size larger than you makes a melee attack roll against you, you can use your reaction to dodge and use its momentum against it. You gain a bonus to AC equal to your proficiency bonus against that attack, and, if the attack misses, you suplex the creature; you move into its space, and it falls prone in the space you left.

element.name: Tough Mother
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: While you aren't wielding any weapons other than pugilist weapons, carrying a shield, or wearing armour, any bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage you take is reduced by an amount equal to your proficiency bonus.

element.name: Reach Mastery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Reach Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.

element.name: Reach Mastery (1)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Reach Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.

element.name: Reach Mastery (2)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Reach Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.

element.name: Reach Mastery (3)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Reach Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.

element.name: Reach Mastery (4)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Reach Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.

element.name: Reach Mastery (5)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Reach Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.

element.name: Reach Mastery (6)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Reach Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.

element.name: Fortified Position
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: While you are wielding a Reach weapon and aren't restrained or incapacitated, the area within 10 feet of you is difficult terrain for creatures of your choice.

element.name: Pole Vaulter
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You have trained to use your weapon to aid you in vaulting and falling safely. While you are wielding a Reach weapon, if you move at least 10 feet on foot immediately before the jump, your jumping distance is tripled until the end of this turn. In addition, when you fall and are wielding a Reach weapon, you can subtract up to 50 feet from your fall when calculating falling damage. You can't gain this benefit if you are unconscious.

element.name: Superior Strike: Reach
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can perform a superior strike with any weapon that has the Reach property.

element.name: Constant Vigilance
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When a creature enters your reach while you are wielding a Reach weapon, you can use your reaction to make an attack against that creature with the weapon.

element.name: Lunging Strikes
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you take the Attack action on your turn, the reach of attacks you make with Reach weapons increases by 5 feet until the end of your turn.

element.name: Crowd Control
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you hit a creature no more than one size larger than you with a Reach weapon, you can move it up to 10 feet horizontally to an unoccupied space.

element.name: Pike Wall
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: While you are wielding a Reach weapon and you are within reach of an allied creature who is also wielding a Reach weapon with which it is proficient, you gain a +2 bonus to your AC. This effect does not stack.

element.name: Scourge Mastery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Scourge Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it. Like the Reach property, the Scourge property extends your effective attack radius, but through a length of flexible material rather than a long stiff material.

element.name: Scourge Mastery (1)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Scourge Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it. Like the Reach property, the Scourge property extends your effective attack radius, but through a length of flexible material rather than a long stiff material.

element.name: Scourge Mastery (2)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Scourge Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it. Like the Reach property, the Scourge property extends your effective attack radius, but through a length of flexible material rather than a long stiff material.

element.name: Scourge Mastery (3)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Scourge Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it. Like the Reach property, the Scourge property extends your effective attack radius, but through a length of flexible material rather than a long stiff material.

element.name: Scourge Mastery (4)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Scourge Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it. Like the Reach property, the Scourge property extends your effective attack radius, but through a length of flexible material rather than a long stiff material.

element.name: Scourge Mastery (5)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Scourge Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it. Like the Reach property, the Scourge property extends your effective attack radius, but through a length of flexible material rather than a long stiff material.

element.name: Scourge Mastery (6)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Scourge Property. This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it. Like the Reach property, the Scourge property extends your effective attack radius, but through a length of flexible material rather than a long stiff material.

element.name: Day Tripper
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you hit a creature no more than one size larger than you with an attack using a Scourge weapon on your turn, you can immediately use your bonus action to attempt to pull the target off its feet. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or fall prone.

element.name: Get Over Here!
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you hit a creature no more than one size larger than you with an attack using a Scourge weapon on your turn, you can immediately use your bonus action to attempt to pull the target. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 10 feet towards you.

element.name: Superior Strike: Scourge
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can perform a superior strike with any weapon that has the Scourge property.

element.name: Disarming Strike
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a Scourge weapon, you can attempt to disarm that creature. It must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, the creature drops one object (such as a weapon) that it is holding of your choice, and you pull that weapon or object up to 10 feet towards you.

element.name: Long Tether
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you take the Attack action on your turn, your reach for attacks you make with Scourge weapons increases by 5 feet until the end of your turn.

element.name: Vicious Strikes
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: The damage die of your Scourge weapons increases to a d10.

element.name: Wrap
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you take the Attack action while wielding a Scourge weapon, you can forgo one attack to make a special melee attack against a creature within the reach of your Scourge weapon. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = your martial save DC). On a failed save, the creature is grappled, and it is restrained while grappled in this way. The creature can escape the grapple if it or a creature within 5 feet of it uses its action to make a Strength check against your martial save DC and succeeds. While a creature is grappled in this way, you can't attack with your Scourge weapon.

element.name: Shield Mastery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Shield. A shield is typically made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your AC by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.

element.name: Shield Mastery (1)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Shield. A shield is typically made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your AC by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.

element.name: Shield Mastery (2)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Shield. A shield is typically made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your AC by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.

element.name: Shield Mastery (3)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Shield. A shield is typically made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your AC by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.

element.name: Shield Mastery (4)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Shield. A shield is typically made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your AC by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.

element.name: Shield Mastery (5)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Shield. A shield is typically made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your AC by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.

element.name: Shield Mastery (6)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Shield. A shield is typically made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your AC by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.

element.name: Shield Wall
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: While you are wielding a shield and within 5 feet of an allied creature that is also wielding a shield, your AC increases by 2. This effect does not stack.

element.name: Reinforcement
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: While you might not intercept every blow, you can cause minor deflections that decrease their efficacy. While you are wielding a shield, when you take damage from an attack, you can reduce the damage you take by an amount equal to your proficiency bonus (no action required). After you use Reinforcement, you can't use this technique again until the start of your next turn.

element.name: Superior Strike: Shield
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can perform a superior strike with any shield.

element.name: Cover
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: While you are wielding a shield and within 5 feet of an allied creature who is not wielding a shield, you can use your bonus action to grant that creature a +2 bonus to its AC until the start of your next turn. The bonus ends early if the creature is ever more than 5 feet away from you or you are incapacitated.

element.name: Numbing Collision
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When a creature misses you with a melee attack while you are wielding a shield, you can use your reaction to strike its attacking limb, numbing it. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or drop the weapon that it used to make the attack (if any) at your feet. In addition, until the end of its next turn, it has disadvantage on attack rolls it makes, and it can't pick up the weapon while you occupy the space and are not unconscious.

element.name: Bulwark
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You are a wall capable of withstanding any onslaught. While you are wielding a shield, when you take damage from an attack, you can reduce the damage you take by an amount equal to your proficiency bonus (no action required). This effect stacks with Reinforcement, allowing you to reduce the damage you take from an attack by twice your proficiency bonus. After you use either Bulwark or Reinforcement, you can't use either technique again until the start of your next turn.

element.name: Redirect Strike
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you are wielding a shield and you are targeted by a melee attack by an attacker you can see, but before the GM determines whether the attack roll succeeds or fails, you can use your reaction to add half your proficiency bonus to your AC against that strike, potentially turning a hit into a miss. If the attack misses, you can force the attacker to target a different target that is within 5 feet of both you and the attacking creature with the attack. Use the attacker's original attack roll to determine if the attack hits.

element.name: Thrown Mastery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Thrown Property. If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.

element.name: Thrown Mastery (1)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Thrown Property. If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.

element.name: Thrown Mastery (2)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Thrown Property. If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.

element.name: Thrown Mastery (3)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Thrown Property. If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.

element.name: Thrown Mastery (4)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Thrown Property. If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.

element.name: Thrown Mastery (5)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Thrown Property. If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.

element.name: Thrown Mastery (6)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Thrown Property. If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.

element.name: Close-Quarters Combat
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Being within 5 feet of a hostile creature doesn't impose disadvantage on your ranged attack rolls made with Thrown weapons.

element.name: Distant Striker
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: The normal and long ranges of ranged attacks you make with Thrown weapons is doubled. In addition, if you make a ranged attack with a Thrown weapon while hidden, missing doesn't reveal your position.

element.name: Superior Strike: Thrown
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can perform a superior strike with any weapon that has the Thrown property.

element.name: Flexible Attacker
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: If you make a melee attack with a Thrown weapon on your turn, you can make a ranged weapon attack with that same weapon as a bonus action that turn.

element.name: Focused Strike
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: As a bonus action, you can steady your body and mind to give yourself advantage on the next attack roll you make with a Thrown weapon this turn.

element.name: Bullseye
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: The critical threshold of ranged attack rolls you make using Thrown weapons is reduced by 1. If you score a critical hit against a creature with a ranged attack roll using a Thrown weapon, you can choose one of the following additional effects for the target to suffer:

ul.li: The target is blinded until the start of its next turn.
The target drops one object (such as a weapon) of your choice that it is holding.
The target's speed is halved until the end of its next turn.

element.name: Whirling Melee
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: After you take the Attack action and make a melee attack against a creature on your turn, you gain advantage on attack rolls with Thrown weapons against other creatures until the end of the turn.

element.name: Versatile Mastery
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Versatile Property. Versatile weapons can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

element.name: Versatile Mastery (1)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Versatile Property. Versatile weapons can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

element.name: Versatile Mastery (2)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Versatile Property. Versatile weapons can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

element.name: Versatile Mastery (3)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Versatile Property. Versatile weapons can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

element.name: Versatile Mastery (4)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Versatile Property. Versatile weapons can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

element.name: Versatile Mastery (5)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Versatile Property. Versatile weapons can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

element.name: Versatile Mastery (6)
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: Versatile Property. Versatile weapons can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

element.name: Quick Parry
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When a creature makes a melee attack roll against you while you wield a Versatile weapon, you can use your reaction to add your proficiency bonus to your AC against that attack, potentially turning a hit into a miss.

element.name: Weapon Focus
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can use a Versatile weapon as a spellcasting focus, and you can use the hand in which you hold a Versatile weapon to perform the somatic components of spells.

element.name: Superior Strike: Versatile
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can perform a superior strike with any weapon that has the Versatile property.

element.name: Heavy Striker
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you attack with a Versatile weapon using both hands, the size of the weapon's damage die is increased by two steps, rather than one (maximum 1d12).

element.name: Shield Bash
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you take the Attack action and hit with a Versatile weapon while wielding a shield, you can use a bonus action to make a melee weapon attack roll using your shield against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d4 bludgeoning damage and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or fall prone. Creatures more than one size larger than you automatically succeed on this saving throw.

element.name: Swift Striker
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: If you take the Attack action on your turn and only attack with a Versatile weapon wielded in one hand, you can attack with that weapon one-handed again as a bonus action. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage for this bonus attack, unless that modifier is negative.

element.name: Dual Maneuver
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you take the Attack action and hit a creature with a Versatile weapon wielded in one hand while your other hand is free, you can attempt to grapple that target as part of that same action.

element.name: Guard
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: While you wield both a shield and Versatile weapon, your ability to deflect attacks is second to none. As a bonus action, you can grant yourself a +1 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, and opportunity attacks against you have disadvantage until then.

element.name: Very Heavy Striker
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you attack with a Versatile weapon using both hands, your critical hit threshold is reduced by 2.

element.name: Impact
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: This weapon deals double damage to objects and structures.

element.name: Pugilist
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: For creatures that roll a die when they make unarmed strikes (such as the monk), the size of their damage die is increased by one (maximum 1d12) when they make unarmed strikes with a hand equipped with a weapon that has this property.

element.name: Battery Strike
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: With incredible dexterity and speed, you hurl numerous weapons at once. Make a number of ranged weapon attacks equal to 1 plus half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) against targets within the normal range of your weapon. Each attack must be against a different object or creature. You must have available weapons that have this superior strike option on your person with which to make these attacks.

element.name: Flurry
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: In a blistering display of speed, you make a number of unarmed strikes equal to 1 plus half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) against creatures within your reach. Each unarmed strike must be against a different target. You then gain a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn.

element.name: Hail of Ammunition
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You let loose a devastating volley of ammunition at a point you choose within your weapon's normal range. Each creature within a 10-foot-radius sphere centred on that point must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking piercing damage equalling a number of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. You must have at least 10 pieces of ammunition you can draw for the weapon that you use to make this superior strike.

element.name: Hamstring
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: In a vicious attack, you attempt to debilitate an enemy. Make an attack roll against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and takes extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus. In addition, until the end of its next turn, the creature's AC is reduced by a value equal to its Dexterity modifier (minimum 0), and its speed is reduced by a number of feet equal to 5 times your proficiency bonus (to a minimum of 0 feet).

element.name: Piercing Shot
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You fire a shot from your weapon packed with destructive force. It flies in a straight line a number of feet up to your weapon's normal range. Each creature whose space the ammunition passes through must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes piercing damage equalling a number of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. The shot stops early after it passes through the space of a Huge or larger creature, or through an object more than 1 foot thick or with an AC of 17 or higher.

element.name: Reeling Blow
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You unleash the full force of your strength and size in a weighty strike. Make an attack roll against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and takes extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus. In addition, until the end of its next turn, the creature has disadvantage on all attack rolls, and on ability checks and saving throws that use Dexterity or Intelligence, and it can't take reactions. In addition, if this attack causes a creature to make a Constitution saving throw to maintain its concentration, the creature has disadvantage on the saving throw.

element.name: Shield Slam
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You smash your shield forward, throwing a creature within your reach off balance. Make a Strength (Athletics) check contested by the creature's Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check. If you succeed on the contested check, the creature takes bludgeoning damage equalling a number of d8s equal to your proficiency bonus and, until the end of your next turn, melee attacks against the target have advantage.

element.name: Shockwave
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You slam your weapon into the ground at your feet with ruinous force, detonating a shockwave of energy. Each creature within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take bludgeoning damage equalling a number of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus and be pushed 10 feet directly away from you.

element.name: Skewer
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: In a thrusting strike, you attempt to skewer an enemy with your weapon. Make an attack roll against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and is also grappled by you. This grapple ends early if you use the weapon to make another attack or you let go of the weapon. When the grapple ends, the creature takes piercing damage equalling a number of d8s equal to your proficiency bonus.

element.name: Trip
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You perform a wide, sweeping attack that strikes at the footing of enemies around you. Each creature standing within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take bludgeoning damage equalling a number of d4s equal to your proficiency bonus and fall prone.

element.name: Whirling Strike
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: In a blitzing vortex of aggression, you strike at all those around you in a sinuous assault. Each creature within your reach must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes damage of your weapon's type equalling a number of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Success or failure, the creature can't make opportunity attacks against you until the end of this turn.

element.name: Superior Strike
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can choose to make one superior strike in place of one of your regular attacks. You can perform superior strikes in this way a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, but no more than once per turn, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. Like other advanced techniques, when your superior strike forces a creature to make a saving throw, it is against your martial save DC (see page 83).
Each weapon type has a specific superior strike it can be used to perform. This is listed in the Weapon Metadata table on page 86. The full list of superior strikes and their effects are as follows:

element.name: Shield Slam
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You smash your shield forward, throwing a creature within your reach off balance. Make a Strength (Athletics) check contested by the creature's Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check. If you succeed on the contested check, the creature takes bludgeoning damage equalling a number of d8s equal to your proficiency bonus and, until the end of your next turn, melee attacks against the target have advantage.

element.name: Reeling Blow
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You unleash the full force of your strength and size in a weighty strike. Make an attack roll against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and takes extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus. In addition, until the end of its next turn, the creature has disadvantage on all attack rolls, and on ability checks and saving throws that use Dexterity or Intelligence, and it can't take reactions. In addition, if this attack causes a creature to make a Constitution saving throw to maintain its concentration, the creature has disadvantage on the saving throw.

element.name: Battery Strike
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: With incredible dexterity and speed, you hurl numerous weapons at once. Make a number of ranged weapon attacks equal to 1 plus half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) against targets within the normal range of your weapon. Each attack must be against a different object or creature. You must have available weapons that have this superior strike option on your person with which to make these attacks.

element.name: Flurry
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: In a blistering display of speed, you make a number of unarmed strikes equal to 1 plus half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) against creatures within your reach. Each unarmed strike must be against a different target. You then gain a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn.

element.name: Hail of Ammunition
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You let loose a devastating volley of ammunition at a point you choose within your weapon's normal range. Each creature within a 10-foot-radius sphere centred on that point must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking piercing damage equalling a number of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. You must have at least 10 pieces of ammunition you can draw for the weapon that you use to make this superior strike.

element.name: Hamstring
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: In a vicious attack, you attempt to debilitate an enemy. Make an attack roll against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and takes extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus. In addition, until the end of its next turn, the creature's AC is reduced by a value equal to its Dexterity modifier (minimum 0), and its speed is reduced by a number of feet equal to 5 times your proficiency bonus (to a minimum of 0 feet).

element.name: Piercing Shot
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You fire a shot from your weapon packed with destructive force. It flies in a straight line a number of feet up to your weapon's normal range. Each creature whose space the ammunition passes through must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes piercing damage equalling a number of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. The shot stops early after it passes through the space of a Huge or larger creature, or through an object more than 1 foot thick or with an AC of 17 or higher.

element.name: Shockwave
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You slam your weapon into the ground at your feet with ruinous force, detonating a shockwave of energy. Each creature within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take bludgeoning damage equalling a number of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus and be pushed 10 feet directly away from you.

element.name: Skewer
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: In a thrusting strike, you attempt to skewer an enemy with your weapon. Make an attack roll against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and is also grappled by you. This grapple ends early if you use the weapon to make another attack or you let go of the weapon. When the grapple ends, the creature takes piercing damage equalling a number of d8s equal to your proficiency bonus.

element.name: Trip
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You perform a wide, sweeping attack that strikes at the footing of enemies around you. Each creature standing within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take bludgeoning damage equalling a number of d4s equal to your proficiency bonus and fall prone.

element.name: Whirling Strike
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: In a blitzing vortex of aggression, you strike at all those around you in a sinuous assault. Each creature within your reach must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes damage of your weapon's type equalling a number of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Success or failure, the creature can't make opportunity attacks against you until the end of this turn.

element.name: Attached
element.source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms

description.p: You can't be disarmed of this weapon, but donning or doffing the weapon takes an action. You can use a hand equipped with this weapon to hold items, but can't attack with the weapon while doing so. Additionally, you have disadvantage on attack rolls using other weapons held in a hand equipped with this weapon and on Sleight of Hand checks using the hand that is equipped with this weapon.
